_comment: Dynamic Toll Pricing config for I-595 Express reversible lanes — Scenario C. Illustrative I-595 range, scaled from the published 95 Express LOS table (PMC8468808). All params config-driven; nothing hardcoded. Algorithm caps run $0.50–$3.00 to hold free-flow speed ≥45 mph. Operator override slider: $0.50–$10.00 (scope's literal range).

C._comment: LOS C: 19–26 veh/mi/ln — stable, some restrictions. minToll matches B maxToll so the table is non-decreasing across bands.

D._comment: LOS D: 27–35 veh/mi/ln — approaching unstable. minToll matches C maxToll.

E._comment: LOS E: 36–45 veh/mi/ln — unstable flow, approaching capacity

F._comment: LOS F: ≥46 veh/mi/ln — breakdown/congestion; cap at $3.00

current_static._comment: Flat base rate — LOS band ignored. Comparison reference for operators.

moderate_variable._comment: Standard LOS band lookup. High-utilization sections rise; low-utilization get a small reduction.

aggressive._comment: Raises max caps on LOS E/F sections to shed more demand to GP mainline / SR-84. Maximizes revenue at cost of larger demand shift.

operatorOverride._comment: Per-section slider range — the scope's literal $0.50–$10.00 SunPass override band. Pushing above the LOS-table ~$3.00 cap shows an 'override' badge in the UI.

_capacityComment: Express lane capacity in vehicles per hour per lane. I-595 Express: 3 reversible lanes; per-lane capacity ~1,800 vphpl (FDOT HCM-based).
_safetyComment: Mainline utilization fraction above which the safety flag fires — the explicit Scenario B bridge. FDOT 'degraded' threshold from SAFETEA-LU.

demandShedSplit._comment: How shed express demand redistributes: 70% to connected GP mainline, 30% to SR-84 frontage. Config-driven; named in the spec.

mainlineBaseline._comment: Typical-peak mainline volume and capacity per connected segment, anchored to ~1,500 vphpl peak and ~1,800 vphpl capacity so 95% is a realistic threshold crossing.

_cadenceComment: Florida recomputes tolls every 15 minutes (PMC8468808). The 'next recompute' chip is a static label; recompute fires on explicit operator action.
